weeks.weekly_summary: Foundation week establishing aerobic base with 2 sessions on preferred days (Monday, Wednesday). Light volume to build consistency.
weeks.coaching_notes: Recent Strava data shows ~10km over 1 hour (comfortable pace ~6min/km). Starting conservatively at 20km for week 1 to establish routine and prevent overtraining. Athlete is running only 2x per week—prioritize consistency and aerobic adaptation. No hard sessions this week; focus on easy conversational pace.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 2 increases to 24km with introduction of 1 quality session (tempo or fartlek) on Monday to begin lactate threshold development.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Recovery day. Light stretching or foam rolling optional.

days.title: 🏋️ Gym Session
days.description: Fixed gym commitment. Light strength or conditioning work.
days.notes: Do not run on this day
days.coach_analysis: Good commitment to the plan—49 minutes of gym work with an average HR of 111 bpm suggests you kept the intensity controlled, which is exactly right for a cross-training day. This light-to-moderate effort will build supportive strength without hammering your central nervous system before your next running session. You're balancing the training week well.

days.title: ⚽ Football
days.description: Fixed football commitment. Good for aerobic capacity and leg strength.
days.notes: Do not run on this day
days.coach_analysis: Solid execution of your football commitment—you maintained an aerobic-focused effort at 158 bpm average, which is right in that sustainable cross-training zone for building work capacity without compromising your running. The 5.75 km over 68 minutes shows you stayed engaged with good intensity variation typical of football, hitting the aerobic and leg-strength targets you planned for. This session delivered exactly what you needed.

weeks.weekly_summary: Volume increases to 24km with introduction of 1 tempo effort on Monday. Establishing consistent 2-session rhythm with aerobic focus.
weeks.coaching_notes: First quality session introduced: 4km at tempo pace (lactate threshold work). This develops aerobic power without excessive fatigue. Wednesday remains easy for recovery. Athlete should feel comfortable with this intensity—it's 'comfortably hard' (can speak a few words, not full sentences). Cross-training (gym, football, pilates) continues to support aerobic development.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 3 builds to 28km with tempo work progressing to 5km and possibly introducing a longer easy run on one of the non-preferred days if time permits.

days.title: Tempo Effort: 2km easy + 4km @ Tempo + 2km easy
days.description: Warm up with 2km easy. Run 4km at tempo pace (comfortably hard—you can speak a few words but not full sentences, approximately 5:30-5:50/km). Cool down with 2km easy. This is lactate threshold work to build aerobic power.
days.notes: First quality session of the plan. Monitor effort carefully—not a race, controlled comfortably-hard pace.

days.title: 🧘 Pilates
days.description: Fixed pilates commitment.
days.notes: Do not run on this day

days.title: Easy 16km
days.description: Conversational pace run. Longer easy run to build aerobic base and volume tolerance. This is your weekly long run—keep it relaxed and sustainable.
days.notes: Preferred running day. This is the longer easy run of the week—build aerobic resilience.

weeks.weekly_summary: Volume increases to 28km. Tempo work intensifies to 5km; easy runs remain conversational. Building towards peak training phase.
weeks.coaching_notes: Tempo session progresses to 5km at threshold pace—athlete should be adapting well to lactate threshold training by now. Wednesday long run extends to 18km at easy pace. Polarized principle strengthened: 80% of volume at Zone 1-2, 20% at higher intensity. Monitor for cumulative fatigue—if excessive soreness or elevated resting HR detected, reduce next week by 10%.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 4 completes the block at 30km with final tempo session (5km). Prepare for progression into Aerobic Development phase (weeks 5-8 in full marathon plan) with potential introduction of longer tempo or fartlek work.

days.title: Tempo Effort: 2km easy + 5km @ Tempo + 2km easy
days.description: Warm up 2km easy. Run 5km at tempo pace (comfortably hard, approximately 5:25-5:45/km). Cool down 2km easy. This extended tempo bout builds stronger lactate clearance capacity.

days.title: Easy 18km (Long Run)
days.description: Conversational pace long run. Focus on steady, relaxed effort and fueling strategy. For a HM target of 2:00 (9:30/km race pace), hold easy runs at 6:00-6:30/km. This builds aerobic base and prepares for longer efforts in coming weeks.
days.notes: Preferred running day. This is your longest run of the block—maintain composure and negative splits if possible.

weeks.weekly_summary: Final week of 4-week block: 30km total. Maintains intensity while preparing transition to peak training. Tempo work holds at 5km; long run reaches 19km.
weeks.coaching_notes: This is the capstone week of the Base Building/early Aerobic Development block. Athlete has built solid aerobic foundation over 4 weeks (100km cumulative, ~80% easy, ~20% hard). Tempo session mirrors week 3—consolidation of lactate threshold adaptation. Long run extends to 19km at comfortable pace. After this week, proceed to Aerobic Development phase (weeks 5-8 of full 16-week plan) with progressive tempo, fartlek, and building long runs toward 11km at half-marathon pace. Monitor fatigue; if cumulative soreness or mood changes detected, take an extra rest day before resuming.
weeks.next_week_preview: End of 4-week block. Transition into Aerobic Development phase: introduce half-marathon pace segments in long runs, progress tempo work or fartlek, build VO2 max if appropriate. Maintain 2 sessions per week until race preparation intensifies.

days.notes: Preferred running day. Hold tempo effort steady—this is your final hard workout before transitioning to next phase.

days.title: Easy 19km (Long Run)
days.description: Conversational pace long run. Extended aerobic effort to build endurance for half-marathon. Focus on controlled breathing, fueling (water/electrolytes every 20-25min if running over 90min), and relaxed cadence. Target pace 6:00-6:30/km.
days.key_focus: Peak long-run aerobic capacity for block
days.notes: Preferred running day. Longest run of the 4-week block. Execute well to build confidence for race preparation phase.

coaching_overview: This 4-week Base Building/early Aerobic Development block establishes aerobic foundation for half-marathon preparation (2:00 target = 9:30/km pace). Athlete runs 2x per week on preferred days (Monday, Wednesday), respecting 4-hour weekly time budget with polarized distribution: ~80% easy (Zone 1-2), ~20% hard (tempo at Zone 3-4). Cross-training (gym, football, pilates) on blocked days (Fri, Sat, Tue) supports aerobic development without running overlap. Progressive tempo work (4km→5km) builds lactate threshold; long runs scale from 10km→19km to establish endurance base. Total cumulative distance: 102km across 4 weeks. Post-block transition to Aerobic Development phase (weeks 5-8) introducing half-marathon pace segments, fartlek work, and VO2 max development. No taper or race-week messaging—race is 10 weeks away; stay focused on aerobic foundation.
weekly_summary: Foundation week establishing aerobic base with 2 sessions on preferred days (Monday, Wednesday). Light volume to build consistency.
coaching_notes: Recent Strava data shows ~10km over 1 hour (comfortable pace ~6min/km). Starting conservatively at 20km for week 1 to establish routine and prevent overtraining. Athlete is running only 2x per week—prioritize consistency and aerobic adaptation. No hard sessions this week; focus on easy conversational pace.
